user_query: What does the Acme Vector Cache do and when should teams use it?

retrieved_context.text: Acme Vector Cache stores embeddings and retrieval results for repeated semantic search queries. It reduces latency for high-volume RAG applications by avoiding duplicate vector database calls.
Teams should use Acme Vector Cache when their application receives repeated or similar queries, when vector database latency dominates response time, or when retrieval cost needs to be controlled.
Cache entries can be scoped by tenant and expired with configurable TTL policies.

llm_answer: Acme Vector Cache stores embeddings and retrieval results for repeated semantic search queries, reducing latency by avoiding duplicate vector database calls [chunk-1]. Teams should use it when they see repeated or similar queries, vector database latency dominates response time, or retrieval cost needs tighter control [chunk-2].
reference_answer: Acme Vector Cache stores embeddings and retrieval results for repeated semantic search queries. It is useful when repeated queries, vector database latency, or retrieval cost are concerns.
